turns.user_message: I used to get so excited about weekend plans, but lately everything just feels completely flat. It has been a few months now, and I still catch myself looking forward to things that never actually spark any joy when they arrive. I tried picking up my old hobbies again, hoping the old magic would just kick in on its own. Instead, I just sit there going through the familiar steps while my mind stays totally blank. It is frustrating to remember how easy it used to be to just feel something.

The longer this goes on, the more I realize that the spark is just completely gone from my daily routine. I keep waiting for it to fade back in like it always did before, but the days just blur together into one long stretch of nothing. I force myself to show up for things I used to love, but it feels like I am just acting out a script I memorized years ago. There is no real weight to anything anymore, just a dull hum that never really lifts. I miss the version of me who actually cared about the little details.

It has been so long since I felt anything genuine that I barely recognize my own reflection anymore. I try to trick myself into caring by chasing new experiences, but every single attempt just crashes into the same heavy wall of indifference. The contrast between how vibrant my life used to be and this endless gray static is honestly exhausting to carry around. I keep hoping the numbness will finally break, but it just settles deeper into my bones with every passing week. I am just floating through everything, completely untethered from the joy that used to define me.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by contrasting with how things used to be. This has been going on for a while.
